definition.content: I made this fancy app with a bunch of Datadog Actions. It includes most of the services critical to the TechStories architecture. I hope I didn't forget anything!
TechStories architecture diagram...but we've added some new features since then...hopefully Datadog can help you figure out it!

An **invocation** is a single run of a Lambda function.

Invocations are a good indicator of the amount of traffic flowing through your application. More specifically, it can tell you if a function stops executing, which can indicate problems upstream.

This widget shows the sum of all your **Lambda invocations** based on the selected timeframe.
Errors on your Lambda code, configuration limits, or lack of permissions can trigger errors. This widget shows the **error rates** from your Lambda function.
A **cold start** is the first request a Lambda function handles and can take time to initialize, which can impact latency for end users.
Enhanced Lambda metrics reveal the **estimated costs** of each function call, making it easy to track how you are spending their money.

definition.title: Successful executions as a proportion of total executions

definition.content: Each bar represents the **sum of all executions** at that timestamp and the pink shows the **proportion of successful executions** out of the total at that timestamp.

definition.title: Top sessions making requests to buckets
